args.query: CUDA OOM when running notebook 6 on 8GB GPU

content.text:
{"total_count":1,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4605002465,"number":2,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"CUDA OOM when running notebook 6 on 8GB GPU","body":"Hi — I'm hitting `RuntimeError: CUDA out of memory` partway through notebook 6 (transformer fine-tuning) on a single GTX 1080 (8 GB VRAM).

Setup:
- Python 3.10, PyTorch 2.0.1, CUDA 11.8
- Pretrained BERT-base, batch_size=32, max_seq_len=256

Error fires during the second training epoch, after the validation step. Memory usage looks fine at the start of training (peaks around 6.5 GB) but the spike when validation runs pushes it over.

Has anyone landed this on 8 GB? I've seen workarounds in the closed issues but nothing definitive.
","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","user":{"login":"SjJ1017","id":100460031,"node_id":"U_kgDOBfzl_w","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/100460031?v=4","html_url":"https://github.com/SjJ1017","gravatar_id":"","type":"User","site_admin":false,"url":"https://api.github.com/users/SjJ1017","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SjJ1017/events{/privacy}","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SjJ1017/following{/other_user}","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SjJ1017/followers","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SjJ1017/gists{/gist_id}","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SjJ1017/orgs","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SjJ1017/received_events","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SjJ1017/repos","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SjJ1017/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/SjJ1017/subscriptions"},"comments":5,"created_at":"2026-06-06T20:25:08Z","updated_at":"2026-06-06T20:25:12Z","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/rpnr-bench/T07_R02/issues/2","html_url":"https://github.com/rpnr-bench/T07_R02/issues/2","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/rpnr-bench/T07_R02/issues/2/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/rpnr-bench/T07_R02/issues/2/events","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/rpnr-bench/T07_R02/issues/2/labels{/name}","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/rpnr-bench/T07_R02","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0,"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/rpnr-bench/T07_R02/issues/2/reactions"},"node_id":"I_kwDOSzF8xM8AAAABEnrC4Q"}]}

Setup:
- Python 3.10, PyTorch 2.0.1, CUDA 11.8
- Pretrained BERT-base, batch_size=32, max_seq_len=256

Error fires during the second training epoch, after the validation step. Memory usage looks fine at the start of training (peaks around 6.5 GB) but the spike when validation runs pushes it over.

Has anyone landed this on 8 GB? I've seen workarounds in the closed issues but nothing definitive.

Saves ~1.5 GB on optimizer state.","html_url":"https://github.com/rpnr-bench/T07_R02/issues/2#issuecomment-4640267027","user":{"login":"SjJ1017","id":100460031,"profile_url":"https://github.com/SjJ1017","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/100460031?v=4"},"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-06-06T20:25:11Z","updated_at":"2026-06-06T20:25:11Z"},{"id":4640267055,"body":"For the validation spike specifically, wrap `with torch.no_grad():` around the val loop and switch the val DataLoader to `batch_size=1`. The graph retention was the actual culprit on my machine.","html_url":"https://github.com/rpnr-bench/T07_R02/issues/2#issuecomment-4640267055","user":{"login":"SjJ1017","id":100460031,"profile_url":"https://github.com/SjJ1017","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/100460031?v=4"},"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-06-06T20:25:11Z","updated_at":"2026-06-06T20:25:11Z"},{"id":4640267096,"body":"+1 — same problem, same workaround as @above. Would be great to capture this in a \"small GPU\" section of the README so newcomers don't have to dig through this thread.","html_url":"https://github.com/rpnr-bench/T07_R02/issues/2#issuecomment-4640267096","user":{"login":"SjJ1017","id":100460031,"profile_url":"https://github.com/SjJ1017","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/100460031?v=4"},"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-06-06T20:25:12Z","updated_at":"2026-06-06T20:25:12Z"}]

data.content:
Here's my concise summary:

---

## Summary of Issue #2 — "CUDA OOM when running notebook 6 on 8GB GPU"

### (a) Root Causes Users Converged On

1. **Computation graph retention during validation** (primary culprit): The validation loop does not wrap its forward pass in `torch.no_grad()`, so PyTorch retains the entire computation graph in VRAM. Comment 4 explicitly calls this out: *"The graph retention was the actual culprit on my machine."*

2. **Oversized default hyperparameters for 8 GB cards**: `batch_size=32` and `max_seq_len=256` with BERT-base leave almost no headroom. Memory peaks at ~6.5 GB during training alone, so any spike (like validation) pushes it over.

3. **High optimizer-state memory**: The default AdamW keeps two momentum buffers per parameter in FP32, adding significant VRAM overhead.

---

### (b) Proposed Workarounds, Ranked by Endorsement Count

| Rank | Workaround | Endorsements | Details |
|------|-----------|:------------:|---------|
| 1 | **Wrap val loop in `torch.no_grad()` + reduce val batch_size** | 2 (comments 4 & 5) | Prevents graph retention; comment 5 says *"same workaround as @above"* and explicitly asks for it in the README. |
| 2 | **`model.gradient_checkpointing_enable()`** | 1 (comment 2) | Drops peak memory ~35% at ~15% training speed cost. |
| 3 | **Reduce `batch_size` to 8 and `max_seq_len` to 128** | 1 (comment 1) | Works but "convergence got noisier." |
| 4 | **`bitsandbytes` 8-bit AdamW** | 1 (comment 3) | Saves ~1.5 GB on optimizer state; adds a dependency. |

---

### (c) README Recommendation

**Add the `torch.no_grad()` validation fix first.** It should go in a new **"Small-GPU Tips (≤ 8 GB)"** section because:

- It directly addresses the *identified root cause* (graph retention), not just a symptom.
- It is the simplest change — a one-line context manager — with zero external dependencies or training-quality trade-offs.
- It is the workaround that two commenters converged on and the one explicitly requested for the README.

As a secondary recommendation, note `gradient_checkpointing_enable()` as a next step for users who still need headroom after the `no_grad()` fix, since it offers the largest memory reduction (~35%) among the remaining options.
